title: The Tipping Point
author: Malcolm Gladwell
subline: How little things can make a big difference.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- An epidemic curve: a hairline curve that sits flat at 0 for the first 2/3 of the x-axis, then EXPLODES upward in the final 1/3. The inflection point where the curve takes off is marked with a RED 2.5px vertical line. Title sits overlaid. -->
  <g transform="translate(160 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Axes -->
    <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="-700" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="1280" y2="0" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- The curve: flat then exponential -->
    <path d="M 0 -10 L 400 -10 C 600 -20 700 -80 800 -250 C 900 -420 1000 -550 1100 -650 C 1150 -680 1200 -700 1280 -700" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2.5"/>
    <!-- Filled region under the curve -->
    <path d="M 0 -10 L 400 -10 C 600 -20 700 -80 800 -250 C 900 -420 1000 -550 1100 -650 C 1150 -680 1200 -700 1280 -700 L 1280 0 L 0 0 Z" fill="#cccccc" opacity="0.06"/>
    <!-- The TIPPING POINT — a vertical RED line at the inflection -->
    <line x1="800" y1="0" x2="800" y2="-700" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
    <circle cx="800" cy="-250" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <!-- A label -->
    <text x="800" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.3em">THE TIPPING POINT</text>
    <!-- Tick marks on x-axis -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1">
      <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="12"/>
      <line x1="400" y1="0" x2="400" y2="12"/>
      <line x1="800" y1="0" x2="800" y2="12"/>
      <line x1="1100" y1="0" x2="1100" y2="12"/>
      <line x1="1280" y1="0" x2="1280" y2="12"/>
    </g>
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="0.04em">
      <text x="0" y="32">T0</text>
      <text x="400" y="32">T1</text>
      <text x="800" y="32" fill="#fc1c46">T2</text>
      <text x="1100" y="32">T3</text>
      <text x="1280" y="32">T4</text>
    </g>
    <!-- A small red arrow at the steepest part of the curve -->
    <line x1="1100" y1="-650" x2="1280" y2="-700" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <polyline points="1260 -690 1280 -700 1260 -710" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The book IS an epidemic curve. A literal infection-curve graph: x-axis = time, y-axis = cases. The curve sits flat at zero for the first 2/3 of the timeline, then explodes upward exponentially. The inflection point — THE TIPPING POINT — is marked with a vertical RED 2.5px dashed line and a red dot. A red arrow shows the steep ascent. Title sits overlaid in the middle, weight 500 command, mixed-case. Brand 'EPIDEMIC' in the bottom-right; pill '100' at the top — Gladwell's 'magic number' for trend adoption.
